key_insight: Gradient noise should be weighted by curvature, because errors in high-curvature directions matter less; this yields a more realistic noise measure for SGD scheduling.
engineering_relevance: CWGD-Cosine can improve SGD training stability and final error with minimal overhead in the quadratic setting, making learning-rate schedules more geometry-aware.
method: Define Curvature-Weighted Gradient Diversity (CWGD) by scaling per-sample gradient diversity with the inverse square root of the Hessian.
Use a CWGD-modulated cosine learning-rate schedule to lower the effective optimization noise floor.
Estimate diagonal curvature with a Hutchinson-based Hessian estimator; exact for quadratic objectives.
evidence_signal: On quadratic objectives across varying condition numbers, batch sizes, and noise structures, CWGD-Cosine achieved about 20% lower final optimization error than standard cosine annealing and up to a 2x reduction in asymptotic error floor.
watch_item: The approach may be sensitive to Hessian staleness in non-convex settings, so curvature estimates must be updated carefully.
paper_title: Curvature-Weighted Gradient Diversity: A Noise Measure for Geometry-Adaptive SGD Schedules
